title: Moonfind Research Studio
query: (all:humanoid AND all:robot) OR (all:whole-body AND cat:cs.RO) OR (all:vision-language-action AND cat:cs.RO)

papers.title: Memory World Models for Long-Horizon Humanoid Control
papers.abstract_text: A recurrent world model predicts embodied state and supports long-horizon humanoid locomotion under partial observation.

papers.title: Contact-Aware Whole-Body Policies for Humanoid Manipulation
papers.abstract_text: We present a humanoid whole-body policy that combines manipulation and locomotion with contact-aware action tokens and simulation pretraining.

papers.title: Vision-Language-Action Transfer with Teleoperated Robot Data
papers.abstract_text: We train a vision-language-action foundation model on teleoperation demonstrations and study cross-robot transfer for embodied manipulation.

summaries.one_sentence: Contact-aware action tokens couple humanoid locomotion and manipulation in one whole-body policy.
summaries.problem: Humanoid mobile manipulation fails when contact and balance are planned independently.
summaries.method: Simulation-pretrained whole-body policy with contact-aware action tokens.
summaries.evidence: The supplied abstract reports combined manipulation and locomotion with contact-aware tokens.
limitations: The supplied evidence does not report hardware robustness or dataset scale.

claims.statement: The paper proposes contact-aware tokens for a combined whole-body policy.
claims.counterevidence: No quantitative result is available in the supplied abstract.

open_questions: Do contact-aware tokens improve recovery on hardware?

summaries.one_sentence: A recurrent world model supplies memory for long-horizon humanoid control under partial observation.
summaries.problem: Reactive policies cannot preserve task state through partial observations.
summaries.method: Recurrent latent world model for embodied-state prediction and locomotion control.
summaries.evidence: The supplied abstract states that the model predicts embodied state and supports long-horizon locomotion.
limitations: The supplied evidence does not establish manipulation transfer.

claims.statement: The paper uses recurrent state prediction for partially observed humanoid locomotion.
claims.counterevidence: No cross-task or manipulation evidence appears in the supplied abstract.

open_questions: Can the memory state encode contact history useful for manipulation?

summaries.one_sentence: Teleoperation data trains a vision-language-action model intended to transfer manipulation across robots.
summaries.problem: Robot-specific demonstration data limits embodied policy reuse.
summaries.method: Vision-language-action pretraining over teleoperation demonstrations with cross-robot transfer evaluation.
summaries.evidence: The supplied abstract reports teleoperation training and cross-robot transfer study.
limitations: Humanoid balance and whole-body contact are not established by the supplied evidence.

claims.statement: The paper studies cross-robot transfer from teleoperated manipulation data.
claims.counterevidence: The supplied abstract does not claim humanoid locomotion transfer.

open_questions: Which action representation best transfers to torque-controlled humanoids?

ideas.title: Contact-memory VLA for long-horizon humanoid mobile manipulation
ideas.thesis: A hierarchical model can preserve semantic task intent while a recurrent contact memory stabilizes whole-body action chunks.
ideas.mechanism: A frozen vision-language planner emits subgoals; a recurrent world-state module compresses proprioception and contact history; a whole-body decoder emits short contact-aware action chunks.
ideas.cross_paper_bridge: It combines contact-aware whole-body tokens, recurrent partial-observation memory, and teleoperation-trained VLA transfer instead of treating them as separate policies.
ideas.novelty_claim: The proposal is the falsifiable bridge between these supplied mechanisms; it is not a claim that the combined model has already been tested.
ideas.counter_hypothesis: A memoryless whole-body policy conditioned directly on VLA embeddings performs equally well and is easier to optimize.
risks: VLA subgoals may be dynamically infeasible
Teleoperation actions may not map across morphology
ideas.falsifier: Reject the recurrent bridge if it fails to improve held-out long-horizon task completion by at least 8 percentage points over the memoryless matched-compute baseline.
ideas.next_experiment: Build a three-task simulation benchmark and compare frozen-VLA plus memoryless decoder against frozen-VLA plus recurrent contact memory under matched seeds and compute.

domain_payload.target_capability: Long-horizon humanoid mobile manipulation under partial observation
domain_payload.backbone: Frozen vision-language encoder plus recurrent latent world model and whole-body action decoder
domain_payload.memory_state: Recurrent latent state updated from proprioception, contacts, visual tokens, and prior action chunk
domain_payload.action_representation: Eight-step whole-body action chunks with contact phase, base twist, joint targets, and gripper command
domain_payload.simulation_plan: Randomize friction, delay, payload, camera pose, and contact noise across three long-horizon tasks.
required_ablations: replace VLA subgoals with task IDs
domain_payload.falsification_metric: Less than 8 percentage-point completion improvement over a matched-compute memoryless baseline

questions.question: Can recurrent memory make contact-aware action tokens more stable across multi-stage mobile-manipulation tasks?
missing_evidence: Matched-seed comparison with and without contact-history memory
questions.next_check: Run a controlled simulation ablation before collecting more hardware data.

questions.question: Can a VLA semantic plan be adapted into contact-safe whole-body action chunks?
questions.next_check: Train a frozen-VLA adapter against the whole-body baseline.
